text:
Git branching is a powerful feature in version control that helps teams work on different tasks simultaneously. A common workflow uses feature branches, where each developer creates a separate branch for a new feature or bug fix. This keeps the main branch stable and free from incomplete code.

Once a feature is ready, the developer submits a pull request to propose merging their changes into the main branch. Other team members review the code, discuss improvements, and approve the changes. After approval, the branch is merged, updating the main project.

This structured process ensures cleaner code, fewer conflicts, and better collaboration among team members.
